figures.caption: Figure 7: Heatmap representations of module aggregation. The first four columns each represent the individual co-occurence matrices of PAPER, DOMINO, HotNet2, and FDRnet respectively. The fifth column is the sum of the heatmaps. The top left corner contains a cluster made up of modules from all four algorithms in the TNFa dataset.
Figure 3: Distribution of module sizes summed across all datasets. n denotes total number of modules. Median is marked.

Figure 6: Heatmap representations of similarity matrices for each dataset. Darker colors indicate higher similarity. The upper diagonal represents the matching similarity (Definition 2) and the lower diagonal represents the sum similarity (Definition 3).
Figure 9: Venn diagrams to visualize GCM. Overlap indicates modules merged together.
Figure 4: mEHR distributions per algorithm and dataset. “NE” (red bar) stands for no enrichment. Slashed bar with number indicates overflow.

Figure 10: Eigenvalues of the normalized Laplacian for each dataset. We choose the number of clusters to be the index of the eigenvalue that maximizes the difference in consecutive eigenvalues. We set k∗ = 5, 13 for TNFa and Fly Transcriptome, respectively.

Figure 8: Module cluster in the TNFa dataset, identified by the module aggregation method, as shown in figure 7. Genes with multiple colors belong to multiple modules. Notably, this cluster is not identified by the conductance merging algorithm (Algorithm 1).

Figure 5: Similar modules identified by different algorithms. A: PAPER module and HotNet2 module in the Fly Transcriptome dataset, B: PAPER module and HotNet2 module in the Aneuploidy1 dataset, C: PAPER module and DOMINO module in the TNFa dataset.
